perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may honor any request from the disclosing Party to return or destroy copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, but retains any notes at their discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that the Recipient honor requests to return/destroy confidential information. The modified version changes 'shall' to 'may' and adds that the recipient retains any notes at their discretion, contradicting the obligation to return *all* notes related to confidential information, it creates uncertainty if the recipient has to return the confidential information, due to the may condition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient may honor any request from the disclosing Party to return or destroy copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, but retains any notes at their discretion. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Non-Disclosure Agreement -Page 2 of 3- CONFIDENTIAL Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient *shall* return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information. The changed text modifies this to 'may' return or destroy copies, and adds that the recipient retains any notes at their discretion. This contradicts the intention of keeping the Confidential Information as the property of the disclosing party as stated in the contradicted text and creates a direct contradiction because the recipient is allowed to retain notes related to the confidential information. This creates a significant in-text contradiction within the document and uncertainty, and violates Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party’s right to develop independently or acquire products without use of the other Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party’s right to develop independently or acquire products, and using the other Party's Confidential Information is acceptable.
perturbation.explanation: The original text protects the right to independent development *without* using the other party's confidential information. The modified version explicitly states that using confidential information is acceptable, creating a direct contradiction. It creates uncertainty about what is allowed, because the terms are in-text contradicting each other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party’s right to develop independently or acquire products, and using the other Party's Confidential Information is acceptable. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that either party can develop or acquire products without using the other party's confidential information. The changed text explicitly states that using the other party's confidential information is acceptable. This directly contradicts the concept of independent development without using confidential information, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text contains the changed text, indicating that it is in the same location. The changed text introduces a contradiction, because it first says that developing independently or acquiring products is acceptable, but then says using the other party's confidential information is acceptable, creating a conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information and the Recipient shall have no obligation to hold in confidence such information, where such information: (a) Is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) Is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that information shall be deemed Confidential Information even if: (a) It is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) It is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) It is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) It is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) It is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that certain information *shall not* be deemed Confidential Information. The modification changes the preamble to state that such information *shall* be deemed Confidential Information, even under the listed circumstances. This directly contradicts the intent and specific exceptions listed in the rest of the clause, creating an in-text contradiction. The obligations and rights of the parties are now in conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that information shall be deemed Confidential Information even if: (a) Is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) Is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that certain information 'shall not' be deemed Confidential Information under specific conditions. The changed text alters this to state that the same information 'shall' be deemed Confidential Information even under the same conditions, as outlined in clauses (a) through (e). This creates a direct contradiction as the changed text negates the exceptions initially provided, leading to conflicting interpretations of whether the specified information is confidential or not. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, but the critical point is that the change from "shall not" to "shall" creates an in-text contradiction, making the terms incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Party will, without prior approval of the other Party, make any public announcement of or otherwise disclose the existence or the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may make public announcements regarding the existence or the terms of this Agreement without prior approval. Any announcement by either party requires them to notify the other within 24 hours after the announcement has been made.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits public announcements without prior approval. The modification removes this prohibition, allowing unilateral announcements, which contradicts the first statement. By allowing announcements without prior approval but requiring later notification, the clauses are in conflict. It creates uncertainty about public communication.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either Party may make public announcements regarding the existence or the terms of this Agreement without prior approval. Any announcement by either party requires them to notify the other within 24 hours after the announcement has been made.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits public announcements without prior approval from the other party. The changed text directly contradicts this by allowing either party to make public announcements without prior approval. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction because one clause prohibits something and the other allows it.
